title: ilex lonicerifolia var. hakkuensis
intro: Ilex lonicerifolia var. hakkuensis, also known as Hallyeon-hoe in Korean, is a rare and endangered species of evergreen tree in the Ilex genus, native to Korea. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, and have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Ilex lonicerifolia var. hakkuensis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help protect against chronic diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative disorders. In traditional Korean medicine, the leaves are often prepared as a tea or infused in water to extract their medicinal properties, and are used to treat conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
